title: ToT vs Chain of Thought
concept: When tree search improves accuracy for complex logic puzzles versus when it adds excessive cost and latency.

exercises.prompt: Last lesson: what does an AI do when a branch of thinking hits a dead end while searching?

options.text: It backtracks and tries a different branch
It gives up and returns nothing
It deletes the whole tree and starts life over

exercises.explain: Search algorithms like depth-first or breadth-first let the AI backtrack, stepping back to an earlier point and trying a different path when one branch fails.

cards.title: One Path or Many?
cards.body: Chain of Thought writes out one line of reasoning, step by step. Tree of Thoughts explores many branches and picks the best. But more thinking isn't always better!

cards.title: The Big Difference
cards.body: Chain of Thought (CoT) follows a single reasoning path forward. Tree of Thoughts (ToT) branches into many paths, compares them, and can backtrack. ToT is more powerful but much slower and pricier.

cards.title: GPS vs Guessing
cards.body: CoT is like driving straight to a destination on one route. ToT is like exploring several routes and picking the fastest. Exploring is great when the map is tricky, but wasteful for a trip down the street.

cards.title: When ToT Wins
cards.body: For a hard logic puzzle with many dead ends, ToT tries branches and backtracks, boosting accuracy. For 'What is 2+2?', ToT just wastes time and money that CoT solves instantly.

cards.title: Myth: More Branches Always Help
cards.body: Not true! Exploring many branches costs more time and money (latency and tokens). On simple tasks, ToT adds cost without improving the answer at all.

cards.title: CoT vs ToT
cards.body: Pick the method that matches the problem's difficulty.

left.text: One straight path. Fast and cheap. Great for simple or clear problems.

right.text: Many branches, compares and backtracks. Slow and costly. Great for hard puzzles.

exercises.prompt: When is Tree of Thoughts the BETTER choice over Chain of Thought?

options.text: A complex logic puzzle with many dead ends
Repeating a word back to the user

exercises.explain: ToT shines on hard problems with many possible paths and dead ends, where exploring and backtracking find better answers. On easy tasks it just wastes time and money.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Tree of Thoughts is always faster and cheaper than Chain of Thought.
exercises.explain: False. ToT explores many branches, so it uses more time (latency) and more tokens (cost). CoT's single path is usually faster and cheaper.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank about the trade-off.
exercises.sentence: Tree of Thoughts can boost accuracy on hard puzzles but adds extra cost and latency.
exercises.explain: Latency means delay. Because ToT explores many branches before answering, it adds latency (and cost), which is only worth it for genuinely hard problems.

exercises.prompt: Which explanation of when to use ToT sounds AI-generated (smooth and balanced)?

options.text: Choose Tree of Thoughts when a problem has many branching possibilities and dead ends; for simple tasks, Chain of Thought is faster and more cost-effective.
ToT good. use it always lol
idk just try both and see which one feels right man

exercises.explain: The first option is polished, balanced, and uses precise terms like 'cost-effective', typical of AI writing. The others are casual and vague, more like quick human notes.

exercises.prompt: An AI must answer thousands of simple FAQ questions per minute, plus a few extremely hard scheduling puzzles per day. What's the smartest setup?

options.text: Use fast Chain of Thought for the simple FAQs, and reserve Tree of Thoughts for the hard scheduling puzzles
Use Tree of Thoughts for everything to be safe
Use Chain of Thought for everything, even the hard puzzles

exercises.explain: Match the method to the difficulty. CoT handles simple, high-volume tasks fast and cheap, while ToT's costly branching and backtracking is worth it only for the rare hard puzzles. Using ToT everywhere would waste huge amounts of time and money; using CoT for the hard puzzles risks wrong answers. Smart agents pick the right tool for each job.
